name: Expression
tagline: Open mics, poetry, comedy, drag. Nothing is off the table.
cardBody: Open-mic nights, poetry, comedy, drag, the occasional reading. Nothing is off the table; everything is by appointment with the door.
problem: Spoken-word, poetry, drag, and stand-up artists in central Kenya rotate through Nairobi for stage time. There is no Nanyuki room that books them on a published night and pays a fee. The result: emerging spoken-word and queer performers travel five hours each way for ten minutes on a mic. The audience that wants the room never gets one closer to home.
approach: Downtown runs a published expression night with paid slots and a curated lineup. Open-mic on rotation alongside booked features. Drag, queer cabaret, and spoken-word treated as core programming, not novelty. Door policy explicit and protective: appointment, dress, behaviour, all on the published door card.

programmes.name: Expression night
programmes.description: Sunday evenings (post-flagship weekend). Three booked spots and four open-mic slots. Paid features, free entry for performers' guests, door-managed crowd.

programmes.name: Quarterly drag and queer cabaret
programmes.description: Booked drag and queer cabaret quarterly. Headliner from Nairobi, two Nanyuki openers, full lighting and sound, paid fees.

programmes.name: Stand-up showcase
programmes.description: Monthly stand-up bill. Three local comedians, one Nairobi headliner. Tape-recorded; clips returned to performers for their reels.

programmes.name: Readings
programmes.description: Quiet-evening literary readings. Local writers, published or not. Open mic for short prose.

outcomes.target: Expression night running. [N] performers booked. Drag and queer cabaret series live.
Stand-up monthly. Readings bi-monthly. First touring spoken-word artist hosted.
Festival week includes a poetry-and-comedy day. Recordings released as a Downtown podcast.
Co-produced spoken-word anthology released. Festival edition #1 features a queer-cabaret late.
Downtown becomes a published, paid stop on the East African spoken-word and queer cabaret circuits.

fundingNeeds.label: Feature performer fees (booked slots, expression night)
Quarterly drag and queer cabaret production
Recording rig for stand-up and readings (audio, two cameras)
Door staff training and venue safer-space programme

partners: [Partner: Hivos East Africa, freedom of expression grant]
